distractors.whyWrong: Reads 手 with kun'yomi (て) instead of on'yomi (しゅ).
Drops the long vowel in 段, reading だん as たん.
Confuses 段 (だん) with 台 (だい), misidentifying the final kanji.

explanation: 手段 means a means or way to achieve something; 手 is read しゅ (on'yomi) and 段 is read だん (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Reads 少 with on'yomi (しょう) instead of kun'yomi (すこし).
Misreads 少 as すく, dropping the final し sound.
Confuses the particle も with や, changing the final particle.

explanation: 少しも means not one bit or anything of, used in negative contexts; 少 is read すこし (kun'yomi) and も is the particle も.

distractors.whyWrong: Confuses 躍 (やく) with 駅 (えき), misidentifying the final kanji.
Reads 躍 as いく instead of やく, dropping the ゃ sound.
Reads 活 with wrong on'yomi (こう) instead of かつ.

explanation: 活躍 means activity or active participation; 活 is read かつ (on'yomi) and 躍 is read やく (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Reads 先 with kun'yomi (さき) instead of on'yomi (せん).
Reads 日 as にち instead of じつ, dropping the voicing.
Confuses 日 (じつ) with 美 (び), misidentifying the final kanji.

explanation: 先日 means the other day or a few days ago; 先 is read せん (on'yomi) and 日 is read じつ (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Reads 並 with on'yomi (へい) instead of kun'yomi (なみ).
Confuses 並 (なみ) with the verb 並べる (ならべる), misidentifying the reading.
Confuses 並 with 靡 (なびき), misidentifying the kanji due to visual similarity.

explanation: 並 means medium quality or ordinary when used as a modifier; 並 is read なみ (kun'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Confuses 弱 (よわ) with 女 (よめ), misreading the left radical as female/woman instead of weak.
Uses on'yomi じゃく for 弱 instead of the correct kun'yomi よわ.
Substitutes める with みる, conflating the causative suffix with the verb for 'to see'.

explanation: 弱める means to weaken; 弱 is read よわ (kun'yomi) and める is the causative suffix.

distractors.whyWrong: Confuses 察 (さつ) with 切 (せつ), misreading a similar-looking kanji component.
Misreads 診 (しん) as さん, using a different on'yomi that conflicts with 診's actual reading.
Voices the さつ to ざつ, adding incorrect voicing to the second syllable.

explanation: 診察 means a medical examination; 診 is read しん (on'yomi) and 察 is read さつ (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Confuses 能 (のう) with 力 (りょく), misreading the right component as strength instead of ability.
Misreads 知 (ち) as し, using an alternative but incorrect on'yomi for this kanji.
Drops the long vowel う from のう, creating ろう instead of のう.

explanation: 知能 means intelligence or brains; 知 is read ち (on'yomi) and 能 is read のう (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 康 (こう) as かう, confusing the kanji's on'yomi reading.
Misreads 健 (けん) as けな, dropping the ん and adding a different vowel.
Confuses 健 (けん) with 立 (たて), misidentifying the left radical or similar kanji.

explanation: 健康 means health or healthy; 健 is read けん (on'yomi) and 康 is read こう (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Uses on'yomi かん for 勧 instead of the correct kun'yomi すす.
Adds an extra りる suffix, over-inflecting the already complete verb form.
Voices the initial す to さ, adding incorrect voicing to the first syllable.

explanation: 勧める means to urge or recommend; 勧 is read すす (kun'yomi) and める is the standard verb suffix.

distractors.whyWrong: Drops the final long vowel ー from こく, producing a glottal stop instead of the full syllable.
Confuses 告 with 告, misreading こく as こえ due to mishearing the final mora.
Misreads 報 as ほうの by adding an extra syllable.

explanation: 報告 means a report or information; 報 is read ほう and 告 is read こく, giving ほうこく.

distractors.whyWrong: Confuses 的 with 滝, reading てき as たき (waterfall).
Applies incorrect voicing to 目, reading もく as ぼく instead of the correct on'yomi.
Confuses 的 with 鉄, reading てき as てつ.

explanation: 目的 means a purpose, goal, or aim; 目 is read もく and 的 is read てき, giving もくてき.

distractors.whyWrong: Fails to apply the geminate doubling rule, reading いち as full いち instead of the suppressed い of いっぽう.
Uses kun'yomi readings instead of on'yomi: ひと (one person) and かた (person/direction).
Confuses 方 with 辺, misreading ほう as ぽい (somewhat, somewhat like).

explanation: 一方 means on the other hand or meanwhile; 一 is read いち (here suppressed in the geminate), 方 is read ほう, giving いっぽう.

distractors.whyWrong: Uses the on'yomi ぶつ (thing, object) instead of the kun'yomi ほとけ.
Misreads the final syllable, changing ほとけ to ほとき by dropping the え.
Confuses 仏 with a different kanji and applies incorrect voicing and pitch accent.

explanation: 仏 means Buddha; 仏 is read ほとけ in its kun'yomi when referring to Buddha or a Buddhist image.

distractors.whyWrong: Uses the on'yomi が instead of the kun'yomi われ for 我.
Confuses 我 with 吾, reading it as あが instead of われ.
Uses the on'yomi ご for 我 and repeats it, misidentifying the on'yomi and the repetition mark 々.

explanation: 我々 means we; 我 is read われ and 々 is a repetition mark doubling it, giving われわれ.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 人 as にん instead of じん, confusing the voiced version of the mora.
Uses kun'yomi for both characters instead of on'yomi: おんな (woman) and ひと (person).
Applies incorrect voicing to 婦, reading ふ as ぶ.

explanation: 婦人 means a woman; 婦 is read ふ and 人 is read じん, giving ふじん.

distractors.whyWrong: Uses on'yomi for both characters: ぶつ (thing) and ご (language), instead of kun'yomi もの and がたり.
Adds an extra syllable by misreading 語 as かたり instead of がたり, dropping the voicing.
Confuses 語 with 係, misreading がたり as がかり (in charge of).

explanation: 物語 means a tale, story, or legend; 物 is read もの and 語 is read がたり, giving ものがたり.

distractors.whyWrong: Adds an unwanted long vowel to ちょ, incorrectly reading 著 as ちょう instead of ちょ.
Misidentifies 著 as 書 (both are writing-related kanji), which has the on'yomi しょ.
Voices the 者 reading incorrectly, reading しゃ as じゃ due to a voicing error.

explanation: 著者 means author or writer; 著 is read ちょ and 者 is read しゃ, giving ちょしゃ.

distractors.whyWrong: Reads 直 with its on'yomi ちょく instead of the kun'yomi ただ.
Voices the ち in the suffix ちに incorrectly to じに.
Misreads 直 as なお (another kun'yomi of 直) instead of ただ.

explanation: 直ちに means at once or immediately; 直 is read ただ and ちに is the suffix, giving ただちに.

distractors.whyWrong: Reads 囲 as い (on'yomi) instead of the kun'yomi かこ.
Uses the imperative/command form ending め instead of the plain dictionary form む.
Confuses the verb む with ぶ, a voicing and consonant shift error.

explanation: 囲む means to surround or encircle; 囲 is read かこ and む is the verb suffix, giving かこむ.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 狩い (hunt) shares the reading いわい but means hunting, not celebration.
Visual confusion: 岩 (rock/stone) can be read いわ but 岩い is not a standard word form.
Homophone-adjacent: 諺 (proverb) is いわ-related but 諺い is not standard; confuses proverb with celebration.

explanation: 祝い means 'celebration or festival,' and the correct kanji 祝 specifically denotes blessing or celebration.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 薫り (fragrance from smoke/incense) reads the same but uses 薫 (to scent via smoke) instead of 香 (scent itself).
Different reading: 籠り means 'confinement' and is a different word with a similar mora count but wrong kanji.
Wrong grammatical form: this is the verb form 'to smell/emit aroma,' not the noun 'aroma' (香り).

explanation: 香り means 'aroma or fragrance,' and the correct kanji 香 directly means scent or fragrance.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 掛かり (something hanging or cost) reads the same but means an attachment or expense, not a person in charge.
Near-homophone: 架 (to bridge/span) creates 架かり (bridging) instead of 係 (an attendant role).
Homophone-adjacent: 欠 (lacking/absent) creates confusion but is not the correct term for a person in charge.

explanation: 係 means 'person in charge or attendant,' and the correct kanji 係 specifically denotes a role or responsibility.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 陰 (darkness/shade on the shaded side) reads かげ but emphasizes negative darkness rather than neutral shadow.
Visual similarity: 景 (landscape/view) shares kanji components but means scenery, not shadow.
Archaic variant: 蔭 is an older writing related to shade but is not the standard modern kanji for 影.

explanation: 影 means 'shade or shadow,' and the correct kanji 影 denotes the dark area cast by an object blocking light.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 沖 (open sea/offing) reads おき or おき (maritime term) and means the opposite—outer depths, not inner room.
Different word: 置 (to place/set) reads おく but means a placement action, not the interior of a space.
Different meaning: 憶 (to remember/recall) reads おく in some contexts but refers to memory, not physical interior space.

explanation: 奥 means 'interior or inner part,' and the correct kanji 奥 depicts the innermost recess or depth of a space.

distractors.whyWrong: Wrong second kanji: 史 (history) replaces 使; this creates a non-standard noun unrelated to diplomacy.
Wrong second kanji: 宗 (sect/school) replaces 使; 大宗 relates to major schools, not ambassadors.
Wrong second kanji: 司 (administer/manage) replaces 使; 大司 suggests a chief administrator, not an ambassador.

explanation: 大使 means 'ambassador,' and the correct kanji 大 (great) + 使 (envoy/messenger) together denote an official diplomatic representative.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 続ぐ (tsuzuku/continue) reads similarly but means to continue or follow, not to join pieces.
Homophone-adjacent: 嗣 (heir/succeed) reads つぐ but means to inherit, not to physically join or graft.
Homophone trap: 次ぐ (rank next/follow) reads つぐ but means to be next or second, not to join together.

explanation: 接ぐ (ru-verb) means 'to join or graft,' and the correct kanji 接 denotes connecting or touching together.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 詰む (to be cornered/stuck in game) reads the same but means something entirely different.
Visual/radical similarity: 絡 (tangle/twine) looks similar to 包 but means to wrap tightly, not to bundle.
Near-homophone: 納 (deliver/store) replaces 包, suggesting containment but not bundling; same noun form.

explanation: 包み (tsutsumi) means 'bundle' or 'package'—the correct kanji 包 (wrap/enclose) directly expresses the sense of wrapping something into a bundle.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone: 資産 (shisan = assets/property) shares 資 but refers to financial assets, not natural resources.
Homophone trap: 試験 (shiken = examination) shares 資 partially but is a completely different word; 験 vs 源 is a radical confusion.
Semantic distraction: While 自然 (shizen = nature) is conceptually related to resources, it is a different word with different kanji.

explanation: 資源 (shigen) means 'resources'—the kanji 資 (wealth/capital) + 源 (source) together mean the source of material wealth and essential goods.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 天使 (tenshi = angel) shares 天 but 使 (use/send) replaces 候, creating a totally different meaning.
Homophone confusion: 天下 (tenka = the realm/world) shares 天 but 下 (below) replaces 候; means realm, not weather.
Homophone trap: 天体 (tentai = celestial body) shares 天 but 体 (body) replaces 候, creating different meaning.

explanation: 天候 (tenkō) means 'weather'—the kanji 天 (heaven/sky) + 候 (wait/season) together mean the condition of the sky and seasonal weather.

distractors.whyWrong: 集う (つどう) means to gather and relates to groups assembling, but it reads つどう not そろう — wrong kanji for this reading.
Homophone trap: 備わる (sonaわru = to be equipped/furnished) shares the う-verb form but uses 備 (equip) meaning to have something built-in, not to gather.
Near-homophone: 調う (totonōu = to be well-arranged/adjusted) is similar but 調 (tone/arrange) refers to harmony/adjustment, not to gather together.

explanation: 揃う (soroう) means 'to become complete, to be equal'—the kanji 揃 (uniform/gather) expresses the idea of things becoming equal or a group assembling together.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone confusion: 強弱 (kyōjaku = strong-weak contrast) shares 強 but 弱 (weak) creates opposite meaning; this is a noun, not an i-adjective.
Homophone trap: 協力 (kyōryoku = cooperation/collaboration) sounds very similar and shares りょく but uses 協 (cooperate) and 力, meaning joint effort, not strength.
Homophone confusion: 強固 (kyōko = firm/solid) shares 強 but 固 (solid) is an i-adjective meaning sturdy; close to 強力 but different nuance.

explanation: 強力 (kyōryoku) means 'powerful or strong'—the kanji 強 (strong) + 力 (power/force) together express great strength or effectiveness.

distractors.whyWrong: Wrong verb form: 刷り is the nominalized/gerund form of 刷る, not the same grammatical form as the target verb.
Homophone trap: 擦る (suru = to rub/scratch) sounds identical but uses 擦 (rub), meaning friction motion, not printing.
Homophone trap: 治る (naoru = to be healed/cured) shares the る-verb form but uses 治 (cure), meaning to recover from illness.

explanation: 刷る (suru) means 'to print'—the kanji 刷 (print/brush) directly expresses the action of printing or pressing ink onto paper.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 往き (ouki = going/departure, archaic) shares the same reading but 往 is archaic/literary, not standard modern Japanese.
Homophone trap: 逝き (iki = departing/passing away) sounds the same but uses 逝 (pass away), which means death or departure to the afterlife.
Homophone confusion: 息 (iki = breath) is a single kanji homophone but is a noun meaning breathing, not the direction 'going'.

explanation: 行き (iki) means 'going or direction'—the kanji 行 (go) expresses movement or direction, as in 'station-bound bus' (駅行き).

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 開 (open) sounds like 会 but means 'opening' rather than 'meeting/gathering.'
Similar structure: 械 (machinery) is a common kanji in compound words but produces a non-standard reading.
Visual similarity: 快 (pleasant) resembles 会 but creates an unattested word meaning.

explanation: 会員 (かいいん) means 'member' or 'membership,' where 会 (meet/gathering) and 員 (person/staff) combine to mean someone who belongs to an organization.

distractors.whyWrong: 臨む (のぞむ) is an exact homophone meaning to face or attend, but uses a different kanji — homophone trap.
望める is the potential form of 望む (can wish/see), not the plain present tense — wrong verb form.
呑む (のむ) means to swallow and sounds similar but reads のむ not のぞむ — near-homophone with different meaning.

explanation: 望む (のぞむ) means 'to desire, wish for, or view,' where 望 (gaze/aspire) combines with the う-verb ending to express wishing or viewing from a vantage point.

distractors.whyWrong: 遺 (い/ゆい) can be read のこ in kun'yomi, so 遺り could seem like のこり, but 遺り as a standalone noun is archaic — the standard word is 残り.
残す (のこす) is the transitive counterpart meaning to leave something behind, not the noun form のこり (what remains).
残念 (ざんねん) uses the kanji 残 but reads ざんねん and means regret — wrong reading and different compound.

explanation: 残り (のこり) means 'remaining' or 'left-over' as a noun, where 残 (remain/be left) with the り-suffix creates a noun form indicating what is left.

distractors.whyWrong: Waist/lower back is a different body part and doesn't specify the thigh where the soreness occurred.
Knee is a specific joint below the thigh, not the thigh area itself that aches from prolonged sitting.
Arm is a completely different limb unrelated to the sourness from sitting.

explanation: 股 in this sentence refers to the thigh area of the leg that becomes sore; 脚 is a general term for leg/limb that encompasses the thigh and is the natural everyday synonym for this body part.

distractors.whyWrong: Acquaintance denotes a casual or minimal relationship, opposite the deep trust and consultation described.
Colleague refers to a workplace relationship, not the personal and trustworthy intimacy of a close friend.
Benefactor focuses on someone who has done a favor, not on the mutual trust and consultation of close friendship.

explanation: 親友 means 'close, intimate friend'—someone you can discuss anything with and deeply trust; 摯友 is a more formal/literary synonym carrying the same sense of sincere, devoted friendship.

distractors.whyWrong: 縮む (ちぢむ) means to shrink or shorten — the opposite direction of extending.
止まる (とまる) means to stop altogether, not to continue past the scheduled time.
遅れる (おくれる) means to be late or fall behind schedule, not that the meeting itself ran longer.

explanation: 延びる in this context means 'to extend or run longer than planned'; 伸びる shares the same meaning of physical or temporal extension, making it a natural synonym for how the meeting went past schedule.

distractors.whyWrong: Repair addresses fixing something broken, whereas maintenance is the ongoing preservation of working condition.
Modification changes the machine's design or function, not the preservation of its current operational state.
Operation/usage focuses on how to use the machine, not the upkeep costs required to keep it functioning.

explanation: 維持 means 'maintenance or preservation'—keeping something in good working order over time; 保守 is a close synonym referring to the regular upkeep and care required to sustain the machine's condition.

distractors.whyWrong: Profit is a business gain, not personal income; household budget management concerns personal earnings, not company earnings.
Assets refer to accumulated wealth/property, not the monthly flow of income received.
Expenditure/spending is the opposite—money going out, not money coming in as income.

explanation: 収入 means 'income or revenue'—money coming in regularly; 給与 is a direct synonym referring to the monthly salary or wages earned from work, which is what one must track for household budget management.

distractors.whyWrong: Revenue/earnings is money coming in, opposite to cost which is money going out.
Quality refers to the standard of the product, not the financial outlay required to produce it.
Deadline is a time constraint, not a financial measure of what the project consumes monetarily.

explanation: 費用 means 'cost or expense'—the amount of money required for something; 支出 is a synonym meaning 'expenditure or outlay,' both referring to money spent on a project that can exceed budget.

distractors.whyWrong: Kind/gentle describes a personality trait, not the closeness or intimacy of a relationship.
Famous describes public recognition, not the personal intimacy or closeness in friendship.
New describes age or recency, not the intimate, longstanding nature of a close friend.

explanation: 親しい in this sentence means 'intimate or close in relationship'; 近い can mean 'close or near' in the metaphorical sense of emotional/relational proximity, making it a natural synonym for describing a close friend.

distractors.whyWrong: Interesting/entertaining is a positive quality opposite to the frightening, alarming nature of dreadful scenes.
Boring describes a lack of engagement, whereas dreadful describes something that is intensely frightening.
Troubled/inconvenienced describes a mild problem, not the intense fear evoked by dreadful content.

explanation: 恐ろしい means 'terrible, dreadful, or frightening'—evoking strong fear or alarm; 怖ろしい is a direct synonym with the same meaning, both used to describe scenes or events that inspire intense fear and dread.

distractors.whyWrong: Office describes an administrative workplace, not a manufacturing or production facility.
Construction site is a project-based workspace, not the fixed facility where cars are manufactured.
Laboratory is for research and experimentation, not the mass production of automobiles.

explanation: 工場 means 'factory or plant'—a facility where goods are manufactured in large quantities; 製造所 is a more formal synonym referring to a manufacturing facility, both describing the place where automobiles are produced on a large scale.

distractors.whyWrong: This means to lie down or recline—the opposite action in this context.
This means to move in general, not specifically the action of getting up from lying/sitting.
This means to walk, which is an action after standing up, not the act of standing up itself.

explanation: 立ち上がる means to rise or get up from a lying or sitting position; 起き上がる is a close synonym meaning to get up, especially after waking or lying down.

distractors.whyWrong: This means apparatus or device—a physical mechanism, not an organizational structure.
While this can mean organization, it refers to a group of people, not specifically an administrative structure or system.
This means plan, which is different from the actual structure or system in place.

explanation: 機構 means a system or organizational structure; 体制 (system, structure, regime) is a synonym referring to the same administrative framework causing delays in decision-making.

distractors.whyWrong: This means to push or press, not to strike forcefully as in hitting or punching.
This means to pinch, a different form of physical contact and less forceful than punching.
This means to kick, a different body part and action than punching with the fist.

explanation: 殴る means to hit or punch forcefully; 叩く (to strike, to hit, to beat) is a synonym meaning to hit with force or impact.

distractors.whyWrong: This means a wall, which is solid and built differently from a fence.
This means a gate, an entryway structure rather than a barrier enclosure.
This means a bridge, used to cross over something, not to enclose or prevent escape.

explanation: 柵 means a fence or paling; 垣根 (hedge fence, fence) is a synonym for an outdoor barrier structure that keeps animals enclosed.

distractors.whyWrong: This means area or region, referring to geographical space, not a span of time or project scope.
This means content or substance, what the project contains, not its scope or extent.
This means scale or size, which measures magnitude, not the scope or range of a project's timeline and extent.

explanation: 範囲 means extent or scope; in this sentence referring to the timeframe and breadth of the project, 期間 (period, duration) emphasizes the temporal extent meaning.

distractors.whyWrong: This means to keep or raise an animal as a pet, the action of the owner, not the animal becoming tame.
従う (したがう) means to obey or follow commands, describing submission rather than gradual familiarity.
仕える (つかえる) means to serve or work under someone, not to become accustomed through repeated exposure.

explanation: 馴れる means to become tame or domesticated; 慣れる (to become accustomed, to become familiar) is a synonym expressing the process of becoming accustomed to humans.

distractors.whyWrong: This means foremost or at the front, referring to position rather than importance or primacy.
While it can mean main, it emphasizes ranking or number one, not the comprehensive primary nature of 主要.
This means necessary or essential, which describes importance but is different from the primary/main objective meaning.

explanation: 主要 means chief or main; 主な (main, primary) is a synonym meaning the principal or primary objective of the project.

distractors.whyWrong: This means tall or expensive, not describing quality or excellence.
This means kind or considerate, a quality of service but narrower than 上等 (first-class overall).
This means cheap or inexpensive, which contradicts the 'first-class' meaning of 上等.

explanation: 上等 means first-class or excellent; 優れた (superior, excellent) is a synonym expressing outstanding quality or service.

distractors.whyWrong: Refers only to the face, a part of the body, not the whole body as the sentence implies.
Refers to strength or power, not the physical body itself.
Refers to posture or appearance, not the body as a whole physical entity.

explanation: In this sentence, 身 means the human body, and 体 is a direct and common synonym referring to the entire physical body.

distractors.whyWrong: Interest is a milder feeling, not the intense state of being 'hooked' or obsessed that the sentence conveys.
Liking something does not carry the same intensity of obsession implied by being 'hooked.'
A hobby is a regular activity; it doesn't capture the recent and intense 'hooked' state described.

explanation: In this sentence, 夢中 means to be intensely absorbed or hooked on something, and 熱中 is a perfect synonym meaning to be deeply absorbed or devoted to an activity.

distractors.whyWrong: Travel can include various types of trips but does not specifically mean an outdoor recreational activity like a picnic.
A festival is a public celebration event, not a small outdoor recreational meal with friends.
A sports day is an organized competitive event, not a casual recreational outdoor meal.

explanation: Since ピクニック is a loanword (katakana) for picnic, the native Japanese equivalent 野外 (outdoor/open air) captures the essence of a picnic as an outdoor recreational activity in nature.

distractors.whyWrong: Changes the okurigana ending しい to しく, which is the adverbial form — the kanji 等 is read correctly but the okurigana is altered, making it a different inflected form.
Misreads 等 using its on'yomi とう associated with the meaning 'et cetera/etc.' (など) instead of the kun'yomi ひと.
Misreads 等 as たい, confusing it with the on'yomi of 対 (たい), a visually and semantically adjacent character meaning 'equal/opposite.'

explanation: 等しい means 'equal'; 等 is read ひと (kun'yomi) and combined with the okurigana しい gives the full reading ひとしい.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 付 as つ (a phonetic shortening) instead of the correct kun'yomi づ, dropping the voiced sound づ→つ.
Misreads 気 as け instead of き, confusing the kun'yomi き with the alternate pronunciation け found in words like 気配 (けはい).
Misreads 付く as つける, substituting the transitive verb form (つける) for the intransitive kun'yomi づく, adding an extra mora.

explanation: 気付く means 'to notice or become aware of'; 気 is read き and 付 is read づ (voiced kun'yomi), giving the full reading きづく.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 刷 as さく instead of さつ, unvoicing the final consonant and dropping the small っ mora, confusing 刷 (さつ) with 作 (さく).
Misreads 刷 by voicing さつ→ざつ, confusing it with the on'yomi of 雑 (ざつ), a visually plausible swap.
Misreads 印 as えん instead of いん, confusing the on'yomi of 印 (いん) with that of the visually similar 円 (えん).

explanation: 印刷 means 'printing'; 印 is read いん and 刷 is read さつ, giving the full reading いんさつ.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 減 as けん instead of げん, using the unvoiced form and confusing it with the on'yomi of 券 (けん), a phonetically adjacent character.
Misreads 減 as へん, confusing it with the on'yomi of the visually similar 変 (へん), which also appears in compound words meaning 'change/adjustment.'
Misreads 加 as が instead of か, voicing the initial consonant か→が by confusing it with the on'yomi of 画 (が).

explanation: 加減 means 'adjustment or addition and subtraction'; 加 is read か and 減 is read げん, giving the full reading かげん.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 掴 by associating it with 握る (にぎる), a semantically similar kanji meaning 'to grip,' substituting にぎ for つか.
Misreads 掴 by voicing the second mora か→が, producing つがむ — a plausible voiced/unvoiced confusion for the kun'yomi つか.
Misreads 掴 by confusing the left-hand radical 扌with 束 (そく/たば), producing a misread onset そ instead of つ.

explanation: 掴む means 'to seize or grasp'; 掴 is read つか (kun'yomi) and combined with the okurigana む gives the full reading つかむ.

distractors.whyWrong: Adds an extra mora く after 謝 (しゃ), confusing 感謝 with the word 癇癪 (かんしゃく), a phonetically adjacent but semantically unrelated word.
Misreads 謝 as さ, dropping the ゃ glide and the long vowel, confusing it with the on'yomi of 査 (さ) as in 感査 — a plausible shortening error.
Misreads 感 as がん instead of かん, voicing the initial consonant か→が, a common voiced/unvoiced confusion.

explanation: 感謝 means 'thanks or gratitude'; 感 is read かん and 謝 is read しゃ, giving the full reading かんしゃ.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 体 as だい instead of たい, voicing the initial consonant た→だ, confusing 体 (たい) with the on'yomi of 代 (だい).
Misreads 体 as てい instead of たい, confusing the on'yomi of 体 (たい) with that of 定 (てい), a common error in compound words.
Misreads 全 as せん instead of ぜん, unvoicing the initial consonant ぜ→せ, confusing it with the on'yomi of 先 (せん).

explanation: 全体 means 'the whole or entirety'; 全 is read ぜん and 体 is read たい, giving the full reading ぜんたい.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 下 using the more common kun'yomi した instead of the less frequent kun'yomi しも, which specifically refers to 'lower position' in certain contexts.
Misreads 下 as か (on'yomi げ/か) and retains も, producing かも — confusing the kun'yomi しも with the on'yomi か of 下.
Misreads the second mora of しも as め instead of も, a phonetic substitution confusing the vowel o→e in the kun'yomi しも.

explanation: 下 means 'under, below, or beneath'; in this reading it uses the kun'yomi しも, giving the full reading しも.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 等 as どう instead of とう, voicing the initial consonant と→ど, confusing the on'yomi of 等 (とう) with that of 道 (どう).
Misreads 上 as しょう instead of じょう, unvoicing the initial consonant じ→し, a common voiced/unvoiced confusion for this kanji.
Misreads 等 as たい instead of とう, confusing the on'yomi of 等 (とう) with that of 態 (たい), a semantically adjacent character used in similar compounds.

explanation: 上等 means 'first class or very good'; 上 is read じょう and 等 is read とう, giving the full reading じょうとう.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 器 as け instead of き, dropping the long vowel and confusing the on'yomi き of 器 with the unrelated reading け.
Misreads 器 as ぐ instead of き, confusing 器 (き) with the kun'yomi of 具 (ぐ), a semantically similar kanji meaning 'tool/utensil.'
Misreads 容 as よっ instead of よう, dropping the long vowel う and inserting a geminate っ, a plausible phonetic compression error.

explanation: 容器 means 'container or vessel'; 容 is read よう and 器 is read き, giving the full reading ようき.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 在/有 using the alternate kun'yomi おる (a dialectal/formal verb for 'to be') instead of the correct ある.
Confuses the kun'yomi of 在/有 with いる, the other common Japanese verb for existence, which applies to animate beings.
Misreads the ending of 有 as う (from 合う aう), distorting the correct reading ある by swapping the final mora る→う.

explanation: 在る／有る means 'to exist, to be'; both kanji are read with the kun'yomi ある (a-ru).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 抱 by confusing it with 抱's on'yomi ほう applied incorrectly, then further confusing the initial consonant; a plausible mis-association with the on'yomi reading ほう→かく.
Applies the on'yomi of 抱 (ほう) instead of the kun'yomi いだ, producing a non-standard but phonologically plausible misreading ほうく.
Confuses 抱 with 包 (つつむ), a visually and semantically adjacent kanji meaning 'to wrap/envelop', leading to the misreading つつく.

explanation: 抱く means 'to hold, to embrace'; 抱 is read with its kun'yomi いだ, giving いだく (i-da-ku).

distractors.whyWrong: Applies the on'yomi of 鐘 (しょう) instead of the kun'yomi かね, a common on'yomi/kun'yomi confusion.
Misreads the second mora of 鐘's kun'yomi, replacing ね with き — a voicing/vowel error on the final kana (ね→き).
Confuses the final mora of かね by substituting な for ね, a plausible vowel-row mis-selection (e-row → a-row).

explanation: 鐘 means 'bell, chime'; the kanji 鐘 is read with its kun'yomi かね (ka-ne).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 中 as じゅう (the on'yomi used in words like 中心 when unstressed) instead of the correct on'yomi ちゅう, confusing the initial consonant ch→j.
Misreads 止 as じ (voiced form) instead of し, applying incorrect voicing (し→じ) to the second kanji's on'yomi.
Misreads 中 using its kun'yomi なか instead of the on'yomi ちゅう, a common on'yomi/kun'yomi confusion.

explanation: 中止 means 'suspension, discontinuance'; 中 is read ちゅう and 止 is read し, giving ちゅうし (chuu-shi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 適 by confusing it with 底 (てい) — a phonologically similar on'yomi — replacing てき with てい.
Misreads 快 as こう (the on'yomi of 好 or 効, visually/semantically adjacent kanji) instead of the correct on'yomi かい.
Applies incorrect voicing to 適, reading てき as でき (voicing the initial consonant t→d), a common voiced/unvoiced confusion.

explanation: 快適 means 'pleasant, agreeable'; 快 is read かい and 適 is read てき, giving かいてき (kai-te-ki).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 叫 by voicing the second mora incorrectly, applying a voiced/unvoiced alternation to change け→げ within the kun'yomi stem さけ.
Misreads 叫 using its on'yomi きょう instead of the correct kun'yomi さけ.
Misreads the okurigana character ぶ as ふ, confusing the voiced bilabial ぶ with its unvoiced counterpart ふ.

explanation: 叫ぶ（さけぶ）means 'to shout' or 'to cry out'; 叫 is read with its kun'yomi さけ, followed by the okurigana ぶ.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 月日 using their individual kun'yomi つき and ひ instead of the compound on/kun reading がっぴ, a common on'yomi/kun'yomi confusion in date compounds.
Misreads 月 as がつ (its standalone on'yomi for months, e.g., いちがつ) instead of the contracted form がっ, and び for ぴ — missing the geminate っ and voicing error on ぴ.
Misreads 生 as しょう (the on'yomi of 生 in words like 生徒 shouto is せい, but confuses with 少 or 小 whose on'yomi is しょう) instead of the correct on'yomi せい.

explanation: 生年月日 means 'date of birth'; 生 is read せい, 年 is ねん, 月 is がっ, and 日 is ぴ, giving せいねんがっぴ (sei-nen-ga-ppi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 想 as しょう (confusing 想 with 象 or 賞, whose on'yomi is しょう) instead of the correct on'yomi そう.
Drops the long vowel of 想 (そう→そ), omitting the elongating う — a common error with extended vowel sounds.
Misreads 想 by confusing it with 息 (そく), a phonologically adjacent on'yomi, replacing そう with そく.

explanation: 理想 means 'ideal'; 理 is read り and 想 is read そう, giving りそう (ri-so-u).

distractors.whyWrong: Drops the long vowel of 料 (りょう→りょ), omitting the elongating う — a common error with extended vowel sounds.
Misreads 衣 as え (kun'yomi of 衣 can be ころも, but confuses the on'yomi い with the vowel え), producing えりょう instead of いりょう.
Misreads 料 by dropping the small ょ from りょう, producing ろう instead — a contraction error losing the palatal glide.

explanation: 衣料 means 'clothing'; 衣 is read い and 料 is read りょう, giving いりょう (i-ryo-u).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 日 as か (the kun'yomi used in date-counting, e.g., ようか = 8th), confusing the date-reading か with the day-of-week reading び.
Misreads 曜 by using a shortened or alternate form よび instead of よう, dropping the elongating う from 曜's on'yomi ようü.
Misreads 日 as にち (a common on'yomi of 日 as in 日曜日's 日) instead of the correct reading び in the compound 曜日.

explanation: 曜日 means 'day of the week'; 曜 is read よう and 日 is read び, giving ようび (yo-u-bi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 堀 by substituting the kun'yomi ending, confusing ほり with ほれ (the imperative/alternate kun form of 惚れる), treating the final mora り as れ.
Confuses 堀 with 掘 (which shares the same phonetic component) and then applies an incorrect kun'yomi くつ instead of the correct ほり.
Voices the initial consonant of 堀, reading ほ as ぼ (voicing error は→ば row: ほ→ぼ), yielding ぼり instead of ほり.

explanation: 堀（ほり）means 'moat' or 'canal'; 堀 is read with the kun'yomi ほり.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 判 by confusing its on'yomi はん with ばん (voicing error: は→ば), yielding ひばん instead of ひはん.
Misreads 判 (はん) by substituting the kanji component 片 (へん), a visually similar element seen in 判's left radical area, giving ひへん.
Misreads 批 (ひ) correctly but misreads 判 (はん) as かん, confusing 判 with the visually similar kanji 刊 (かん), yielding ひかん.

explanation: 批判（ひはん）means 'criticism'; 批 is read ひ and 判 is read はん.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 溺 by associating it with 沈 (しずむ, 'to sink'), a semantically related water kanji, substituting しず for おぼ.
Correctly reads 溺 as おぼ but substitutes the okurigana-like ending, confusing れる with える — borrowing the reading of the common word 覚える (おぼえる).
Misreads 溺 (おぼ) by confusing it with 弱 (よわ), a phonetically/visually unrelated but commonly confused kun'yomi substitution, yielding よわれる.

explanation: 溺れる（おぼれる）means 'to drown' or 'to indulge in'; 溺 is read with the kun'yomi おぼ (followed by the okurigana れる).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 終 (しゅう) by voicing the initial consonant, confusing しゅう with じゅう (voicing error: し→じ), yielding さいじゅう.
Drops the long vowel in 終：しゅう→しゅ, a common error of omitting the extended vowel sound, yielding さいしゅ.
Misreads 終 (しゅう) as ごう, confusing 終 with the visually similar kanji 後 or 号, substituting ごう for しゅう.

explanation: 最終（さいしゅう）means 'last' or 'final'; 最 is read さい and 終 is read しゅう.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 加 (くわ) by substituting it with an unrelated kun'yomi, and drops える entirely, confusing 加える with 変える (かえる) — a semantically adjacent word with a similar structure.
Misreads 加 (くわ) as くば, voicing the わ into ば (semi-voicing substitution: わ→ば), yielding くばえる.
Misreads 加 by substituting the on'yomi か for the kun'yomi くわ's initial mora, then retaining わ, producing かわえる — a blend of on'yomi か and the remaining kun'yomi mora わ.

explanation: 加える（くわえる）means 'to add' or 'to append'; 加 is read with the kun'yomi くわ (followed by the okurigana える).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 化 (か) by voicing the initial consonant, reading か as が (voicing error: か→が), yielding さんが.
Misreads 化 (か) by substituting the kun'yomi-adjacent reading け (as in 化ける/ばける, where 化 can be read け in compounds like 化粧), yielding さんけ.
Misreads 酸 (さん) by applying gemination and dropping the nasal, confusing 酸 with 作 (さく→さっ) before 化 (か), yielding さっか.

explanation: 酸化（さんか）means 'oxidation'; 酸 is read さん and 化 is read か.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 中 (ちゅう) by substituting the kun'yomi なか instead of the on'yomi ちゅう, yielding さいなか.
Misreads 中 (ちゅう) by voicing the initial consonant, reading ちゅう as じゅう (voicing error: ち→じ), yielding さいじゅう.
Drops the long vowel in 中：ちゅう→ちゅ, omitting the extended vowel sound, yielding さいちゅ.

explanation: 最中（さいちゅう）means 'in the middle of'; 最 is read さい and 中 is read ちゅう.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 飯 using its on'yomi はん instead of the kun'yomi めし.
Misreads the kun'yomi initial mora of 飯: substitutes ま for め, a plausible vowel-row confusion in kun'yomi recall.
Misreads the second mora of 飯 (し) as じ — wrong voiced/unvoiced consonant.

explanation: 飯（めし）means 'meals / cooked rice / food'; 飯 is read めし using its kun'yomi.

distractors.whyWrong: Drops the long vowel in 揺: よう → よ, omitting the trailing う that extends the vowel.
Misreads 動 on'yomi どう as とう — wrong voiced/unvoiced initial consonant (d→t).
Misreads 揺 (よう) as ゆう — confuses the on'yomi vowel nucleus よ with ゆ, a common phonetic blend.

explanation: 動揺（どうよう）means 'disturbance / shock / being flustered'; 動 is read どう and 揺 is read よう.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads the final mora of 嵐: voices し as じ — wrong voiced/unvoiced consonant.
Misreads 嵐 using its on'yomi らん and adds し, confusing the kun'yomi structure with an on'yomi base.
Misreads the final kanji component of 嵐 by associating the 風 element with か (from 風 kun'yomi かぜ initial mora) instead of し.

explanation: 嵐（あらし）means 'storm'; 嵐 is read あらし using its kun'yomi.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads the okurigana-adjacent reading of 送: substitutes the kun'yomi stem おくれ (as in 遅れ) for おくり, confusing two kun'yomi forms of 送.
Misreads 送 using an on'yomi そう instead of the kun'yomi おく, producing みそうり.
Misreads 見 using its on'yomi けん instead of the kun'yomi み.

explanation: 見送り（みおくり）means 'seeing someone off'; 見 is read み (kun'yomi) and 送り is read おくり (kun'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 賞 by confusing it with the on'yomi of 情 (じょう) — phonetically adjacent on'yomi from a different kanji.
Drops the long vowel in 賞: しょう → しょ, omitting the trailing う.
Misreads 賞 on'yomi しょう by substituting そう — confuses the initial consonant cluster しょ with そ.

explanation: 賞（しょう）means 'prize / award'; 賞 is read しょう using its on'yomi.

distractors.whyWrong: Drops the long vowel in 明: めい → め, omitting the trailing い that lengthens the vowel.
Misreads 明 on'yomi めい as みょう — confuses めい with the alternate on'yomi みょう (as in 明日 あす/みょうにち).
Misreads 文 on'yomi ぶん as もん — confuses two valid on'yomi of 文 (ぶん vs. もん).

explanation: 文明（ぶんめい）means 'civilization'; 文 is read ぶん and 明 is read めい.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 為 using its common on'yomi-based reading する (as in 為る is sometimes glossed alongside する), substituting the more common word form instead of the kun'yomi なる.
Misreads 為 using the alternate kun'yomi ため (meaning 'for the sake of'), instead of なる.
Misreads 為 by confusing it visually with 似 (にる — 'to resemble'), a visually and structurally similar kanji.

explanation: 為る（なる）means 'to become / to be of use / to reach'; 為 is read なる using its kun'yomi.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 上 using an alternate kun'yomi あがる instead of のぼる — both are valid kun'yomi of 上 but あがる is incorrect here.
Misreads the okurigana of 上る: substitutes れ for る, confusing the dictionary form ending with an imperative/potential stem.
Misreads 上 using its on'yomi じょう instead of the kun'yomi のぼ, then appends the okurigana る.

explanation: 上る（のぼる）means 'to go up / to climb / to ascend'; 上 is read のぼ (kun'yomi) with okurigana る.

distractors.whyWrong: Drops the long vowel in 情: じょう → じょ, omitting the trailing う.
Misreads 情 using its alternate on'yomi せい (as in 情け → なさけ, or compounds like 情報 can cue じょう, but 感情 seい reading confusion) — confuses じょう with the alternate on'yomi せい.
Misreads 情 using its kun'yomi なさけ ('sympathy/mercy') instead of the on'yomi じょう.

explanation: 情（じょう）means 'feelings / emotion / passion'; 情 is read じょう using its on'yomi.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads the second 加 kanji component as a simple vowel extension, dropping the わ mora and reducing わわ to ある — incorrect rendaku/compound reading.
Misreads 加 as か (on'yomi) instead of the kun'yomi くわ, producing か for the first mora.
Changes the okurigana ending る to り, substituting a different verb-form ending — invalid misreading of the kanji reading itself is bundled with swapping る→り on わる.

explanation: 加わる (くわわる) means 'to join in / to be added to'; 加 is read くわ (kun'yomi) and わる is the okurigana.

distractors.whyWrong: Substitutes the kun'yomi reading of 横 (よこ, meaning 'side/beside'), a semantically and visually related kanji sometimes confused with 脇.
Substitutes the kun'yomi reading of 傍 (そば, meaning 'beside'), a semantically similar kanji that can be confused with 脇 in context.
Confuses the reading of 脇 (わき) with わけ, swapping the final き for け — a plausible phonological substitution of a similar-sounding mora.

explanation: 脇 (わき) means 'side / flank'; the single kanji 脇 is read わき (kun'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 婦 as ぶ instead of ふ — confuses the unvoiced ふ with its voiced counterpart ぶ.
Substitutes the kun'yomi of 夫 alone (おっと, 'husband') instead of the compound on'yomi reading ふうふ.
Drops the long vowel in 夫's reading ふう→ふ, producing ふふ instead of ふうふ — a common long-vowel omission error.

explanation: 夫婦 (ふうふ) means 'married couple / husband and wife'; 夫 is read ふう and 婦 is read ふ (both on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 妹 as まえ, confusing 妹 with 前 (まえ, 'front/before') — a phonological substitution based on a different kanji.
Substitutes the kun'yomi readings of both kanji individually (姉=あね, 妹=いもうと) instead of the compound on'yomi reading しまい.
Misreads 姉 as じ instead of し — confuses the unvoiced し with its voiced counterpart じ.

explanation: 姉妹 (しまい) means 'sisters'; 姉 is read し and 妹 is read まい (both on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 湿 as じ instead of し — confuses the unvoiced し with its voiced counterpart じ, a common voicing error.
Misreads the kun'yomi of 湿 as しけ (as in 湿気 しけ/しっけ) instead of しめ, confusing it with the alternate reading used in related compound words.
Substitutes the kun'yomi of 濡 (ぬれる, 'to get wet'), a semantically similar kanji sometimes confused with 湿 in meaning.

explanation: 湿る (しめる) means 'to become damp / to be moist'; 湿 is read しめ (kun'yomi) and る is the okurigana.

distractors.whyWrong: Swaps the order of the two kanji readings (し→じ for 思, and い for 意 moved to second), reversing the mora sequence and voicing し→じ.
Misreads 思 as ち instead of し, confusing it with the on'yomi of 知 (ち) — a semantically adjacent kanji.
Misreads 思 as こ instead of し, confusing it with the on'yomi of 己 or 故 (こ) — a plausible on'yomi substitution.

explanation: 意思 (いし) means 'intention / purpose'; 意 is read い and 思 is read し (both on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 一 as いち (its standard on'yomi) instead of the geminate いっ, failing to apply the double-consonant assimilation before た.
Misreads 体 as たり instead of たい, confusing the on'yomi たい with an inflected verb-like ending たり.
Misreads 体 as たん instead of たい, confusing 体 (たい) with 単 or 端 (たん) — similar on'yomi endings.

explanation: 一体 (いったい) means 'one body / what on earth'; 一 is read いっ (with gemination before た) and 体 is read たい (on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 隠 as かけ instead of かく, confusing the kun'yomi かく with かけ — a plausible one-mora vowel substitution (く→け).
Misreads 隠 as がく instead of かく, voicing the initial か→が — a common voiced/unvoiced confusion.
Misreads 隠 as おく instead of かく, substituting the on'yomi of 置 (おく) — a semantically and phonologically adjacent reading.

explanation: 隠す (かくす) means 'to hide / to conceal'; 隠 is read かく (kun'yomi) and す is the okurigana.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 格 as がく instead of かく — confuses the on'yomi かく with がく, applying erroneous voicing to the initial consonant.
Misreads 資 as ざ instead of し — voices the unvoiced し→ざ, a common voiced/unvoiced substitution error.
Misreads 格 as きゃく instead of かく, confusing 格 (かく) with 客 (きゃく) — a visually and phonologically adjacent on'yomi.

explanation: 資格 (しかく) means 'qualification / credential'; 資 is read し and 格 is read かく (both on'yomi).

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 案 as かん (on'yomi of 館/感) instead of the correct on'yomi あん.
Misreads 答 using its kun'yomi こたえ instead of the on'yomi とう.
Misreads 答 as どう (voiced on'yomi confusion with 道/同) instead of the correct とう.

explanation: 答案 means 'examination paper/script'; 答 is read とう and 案 is read あん, giving とうあん.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 点 as てい (on'yomi of 定/帝) instead of the correct on'yomi てん.
Misreads 点 by voicing it to でん instead of the correct unvoiced てん.
Misreads 視 by voicing し to じ, confusing it with 自 (じ) as in 自転 (じてん).

explanation: 視点 means 'point of view'; 視 is read し and 点 is read てん, giving してん.

distractors.whyWrong: Misreads 期 as ご (on'yomi of 後/語) instead of the correct on'yomi き.
Misreads 期 as けい (on'yomi of 計/係) instead of the correct on'yomi き.
Misreads 期 by voicing き to ぎ, a common voiced/unvoiced confusion.

Near-homophone: 従妹 is read じゅうまい (not いとこ) and means 'younger female cousin' in formal usage, a different reading and narrower meaning.

explanation: 従姉妹 means 'female cousin' and is the correct kanji because 従 indicates the cousin relationship, 姉 (older sister) and 妹 (younger sister) together specify the female gender of the cousin.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 勤め is also read つとめ but means 'employment/going to work,' not 'duty or obligation' in the sense of a student's role.
Homophone trap: 努め is also read つとめ but means 'effort/endeavor' (from 努力), which differs from the sense of an assigned duty or role.
Visual confusion: 刀 looks similar to 務 in its radical component but 刀め is not a real word and means nothing in this context.

explanation: 務め means 'duty or responsibility (assigned to a role)' and is the correct kanji because 務 specifically denotes an obligation or task tied to one's position, perfectly matching a student's duty to study.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone: 金子 is read きんす or かねこ (not きんこ) and means 'money/gold pieces' or is a surname, not 'a safe or vault.'
Homophone trap: 禁固 is also read きんこ but means 'imprisonment,' not 'a physical safe for storing valuables.'
Visual confusion: 箱 (box) looks conceptually and visually adjacent to 庫 (storehouse), but 金箱 means 'a cash box' and is a different word from 金庫 (safe/vault).

explanation: 金庫 means 'a safe or vault for storing valuables' and is the correct kanji because 金 (gold/money) combined with 庫 (storehouse) forms the standard term for a lockable security safe.

distractors.whyWrong: 始 is visually similar to 支 but means 'beginning/start', making 始店 a non-word in this context.
Homophone trap: 支点 is also read してん but means 'fulcrum/pivot point', not 'branch office'.
Near-homophone trap: 市 (city/market) replaces 支, creating a word with a different meaning and slightly different nuance.

explanation: 支店 means 'branch office/store', where 支 means 'support/branch' and 店 means 'shop/office', correctly describing a subsidiary location of a business.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 像 looks similar to 様 but means 'statue/image', so 王像 means a statue of a king, not 'king' as a title.
Near-homophone trap: 皇 (emperor/sovereign) is read こう/おう but replaces 王, shifting meaning toward 'emperor' rather than 'king'.
Visual confusion: 旺 looks similar to 王 but means 'prosperous/thriving' and 旺様 is not the established word for 'king'.

explanation: 王様 means 'king', where 王 means 'ruler/king' and the honorific 様 elevates it to a respectful or fairy-tale register, as seen in children's stories.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 一帯 is also read いったい but means 'a whole area/zone', not 'what on earth' or 'generally'.
Homophone trap: 一対 is also read いったい but means 'a pair/one set', not the exclamatory or generalizing sense of 一体.
Near-homophone trap: 一退 could suggest 'one retreat' and is not the standard word for the exclamatory meaning intended here.

explanation: 一体 means 'what on earth / on earth' in exclamatory questions (and also 'one body/generally'), where 体 conveys 'body/substance', making this the correct kanji for the rhetorical intensifier.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 宙 is read ちゅう (not じゅう) and means 'mid-air/space', not 'dwelling'.
Homophone trap: 重 can be read じゅう and means 'heavy/layered', not 'dwelling/living'.
Homophone trap: 充 is read じゅう and means 'full/sufficient', not 'to live/dwell'.

explanation: 住 means 'to live/dwell' and is the correct kanji in 住んでいます (living), representing the concept of residence or habitation.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 頸 looks similar to 頬 (both share the 頁 component) but means 'neck', not 'cheek'.
Visual confusion: 頷 also contains 頁 like 頬 and looks structurally similar, but means 'chin/to nod', not 'cheek'.
Near-homophone trap: 朴 is read ほく/ぼく (not ほほ) and means 'simple/plain', bearing no relation to 'cheek'.

explanation: 頬 means 'cheek (of the face)' and is the correct kanji, specifically referring to the fleshy part of the face that reddens with embarrassment.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 他 is read た/たに in some compounds but primarily means 'other/another', not 'valley'.
Near-homophone trap: 滝 is read たき (not たに) and means 'waterfall', not 'valley'.
Visual confusion: 欲 shares structural elements similar to 谷 but means 'desire/greed', not 'valley'.

explanation: 谷 means 'valley', referring to the low area between mountains through which clear water flows, as described in the example sentence.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 温 looks similar to 穏 but means 'warm (temperature)', and 温やか is not the established word for 'calm/gentle'.
Near-homophone trap: 緩やか is read ゆるやか (not おだやか) and means 'gradual/loose', not 'calm/gentle'.
Visual confusion: 稳 is the simplified Chinese form of 穏 and is not standard in Japanese, making it an incorrect kanji choice for this word.

explanation: 穏やか means 'calm, gentle, quiet', where 穏 conveys tranquility and peacefulness, correctly describing pleasant, mild weather perfect for a stroll.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 武台 is read ぶたい but 武 means 'military/martial', giving a completely different meaning from 'theater stage'.
Homophone trap: 部隊 is also read ぶたい but means 'military unit/troops', not 'theater stage'.
Visual confusion: 代 looks somewhat similar to 台 but means 'generation/era/fee', so 舞代 does not mean 'stage'.

explanation: 舞台 means 'stage (theater)', where 舞 means 'dance/performance' and 台 means 'platform/stand', together forming the word for a theatrical performance stage.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 悲曲 could suggest a 'sad piece of music' (ひきょく) and is not the standard word for 'tragedy' in the dramatic sense.
Near-homophone trap: 秘劇 would be read ひげき but 秘 means 'secret/hidden', making it a different word not meaning 'tragedy'.
Homophone trap: 非劇 is read ひげき but 非 means 'non-/wrong', so it would mean 'non-drama' rather than 'tragedy'.

explanation: 悲劇 means 'tragedy', where 悲 means 'sorrow/grief' and 劇 means 'drama/play', together correctly describing a story or film with a sorrowful, tragic ending.

distractors.whyWrong: 制 (control/system) is visually similar to 静 but 冷制 means something like 'cold control' and is not the word for 'calm'.
令 (command/order) is a near-homophone of 冷 (cold/cool) but 令静 is not the established word for 'calm'.
整 (arrange/tidy) looks similar to 静 but 冷整 means 'cold arrangement', not 'calm composure'.

explanation: 冷静 means 'calm/composed' and combines 冷 (cool) + 静 (quiet/still), making it the correct kanji for the concept of keeping a cool head.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 並 is also read なみ but means 'ordinary/average/row', not 'wave'.
Near-homophone trap: 名 is read な (not なみ) and means 'name', not 'wave'.
Visual confusion: 浪 looks similar to 波 and can also refer to waves in literary contexts, but as a standalone noun for 'wave' in modern Japanese, 波 is the correct form.

explanation: 波 means 'wave (of water)' and is the standard kanji used for ocean or water waves, making it the correct choice in this surfing context.

distractors.whyWrong: 時 (time) is a near-homophone of 事 (matter/affair) but 時件 is not a real Japanese word.
Visual confusion: 見 looks similar to 件 in some scripts but 事見 is not a real word meaning 'incident'.
Near-homophone trap: 辞 (resign/word) shares the じ reading but 辞件 is not a real Japanese word.

explanation: 事件 means 'incident/case/affair' and uses 事 (matter) + 件 (case/item), the standard kanji compound for a newsworthy event or crime.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 感情 is also read かんじょう but means 'emotion/feeling', not 'bill/calculation'.
Near-homophone trap: 歓 (joy) shares the かん reading and 情 can be read じょう, but 歓情 is not the correct word for 'bill/check at a restaurant'.
Visual confusion: 情 looks similar to 定 but 勘情 is not the established word for 'calculation/bill'.

explanation: 勘定 means 'calculation, bill, or check (at a restaurant)' and combines 勘 (intuition/perception) + 定 (fixed/decide), making it the correct word when asking for the bill.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 隅 is also read すみ but means 'corner (of a room)', not 'ink'.
Near-homophone trap: 炭 is read すみ and means 'charcoal', not 'ink used in calligraphy'.
Visual confusion: 黒 means 'black' and appears in the same sentence describing the ink's color, but it is not the word for 'ink' itself.

explanation: 墨 means 'ink (especially the black ink stick used in calligraphy)' and is the correct kanji for the writing ink used in 書道 (calligraphy) class.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 所 (place) looks similar to 書 (book/writing) but 教科所 is not a real word meaning 'textbook'.
Near-homophone trap: 化 replaces 科 and shares a similar sound, but 教化書 is not the established term for 'textbook'.
Near-homophone trap: 強化 (reinforcement) sounds similar to きょうか but 強化書 is not a real word for 'textbook'.

explanation: 教科書 means 'textbook' and combines 教 (teach) + 科 (subject/course) + 書 (book/writing), making it the correct compound for a school textbook.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 死角 is also read しかく but means 'blind spot', not 'qualification'.
Homophone trap: 四角 is also read しかく but means 'square/rectangle', not 'qualification'.
Homophone trap: 視角 is also read しかく but means 'viewing angle/visual angle', not 'qualification'.

explanation: 資格 means 'qualification/credential' and combines 資 (resources/funds) + 格 (status/standard), making it the correct word for the license or certification needed to become a nurse.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 位 is also read い but means 'rank/position/grade', not 'will/intention'.
Homophone trap: 胃 is also read い but means 'stomach (organ)', not 'will/intention'.
Homophone trap: 異 is also read い but means 'different/strange', not 'will/intention'.

explanation: 意 means 'will/intention/mind' and in the phrase 意に反して it expresses acting against someone's wishes, making 意 the correct kanji here.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 建つ is also read たつ but means 'to be built (of a structure)', not 'to pass (of time)'.
Homophone trap: 発つ is also read たつ but means 'to depart/set off', not 'to pass (of time)'.
Homophone trap: 絶つ is also read たつ but means 'to cut off / sever', not 'to pass (of time)'.

explanation: 経つ means '(time) passes'; 経 carries the meaning of 'passing through / elapsing', making it the correct kanji for the flow of time.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 鳴らす is also read ならす but means 'to sound / ring (a bell)', not 'to accustom'.
Homophone trap: 均す is also read ならす but means 'to level / smooth out (a surface)', not 'to accustom'.
Near-homophone trap: 倣う (ならう) means 'to imitate/follow', and 倣す is a near-homophone form, but it means imitating, not accustoming.

explanation: 慣らす means 'to accustom / break in'; 慣 carries the meaning of 'habit / familiarity', making it the correct kanji when getting used to something new.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 公演 is also read こうえん but means 'public performance (theatrical/musical)', not 'lecture or talk'.
Homophone trap: 公園 is also read こうえん but means 'park', not 'lecture or talk'.
Visual confusion: 縁 looks similar to 演 in brushstroke structure, but 講縁 is not the word for a lecture; 演 (performance/presentation) is the correct second character.

explanation: 講演 means 'a formal lecture or talk'; 講 means 'to lecture/explain' and 演 means 'to perform/present', together correctly expressing a public speech or talk.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 哀 (sorrow/grief) looks similar to 愛 in some components, and while 可哀そう is actually an accepted alternate spelling, this card's correct target is 可愛そう as given.
Visual confusion: 河 (river) is visually similar to 可 (can/passable) but 河愛そう is not a real word and carries no meaning of pitiability.
Visual confusion: 仮 (temporary/provisional) resembles 可 in shape, but 仮愛そう is not a real word and does not mean 'poor or pitiable'.

explanation: 可愛そう means 'poor / pitiable'; 可愛 normally means 'cute', but here with そう it expresses sympathy toward someone in a pitiful situation.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 額 is also read がく but means 'amount of money / framed picture', not 'learning or knowledge'.
Near-homophone trap: 楽 is read がく (or らく) and means 'music / comfort/ease', not 'learning or academic study'.
Homophone trap: 岳 is also read がく but means 'mountain peak', not 'learning or knowledge'.

explanation: 学 means 'learning / academic study'; it is the correct kanji representing scholarly knowledge, as seen in compounds like 経済学 (economics).

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 語 is also read ご but means 'language / word', not 'afterwards / since then'.
Near-homophone trap: 期 is read き (or ご in limited compounds) and means 'period of time / term', not 'afterwards'.
Visual confusion: 侯 (feudal lord/marquis) is visually similar to 後 due to shared stroke elements on the right side, but it carries no meaning of 'afterwards'.

explanation: 後 means 'afterwards / since then'; in その後 it marks a point in time after a previous event, and 後 is the standard kanji for 'after' or 'later'.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 買 (to buy) looks similar to 売 (to sell) and shares components with 売, but 商買 is not a real Japanese word for trade or business.
Visual confusion: 尚 is visually similar to 商 in its upper portion, but 尚売 is not a real word and carries no meaning of commerce or business.
Visual confusion: 渉 (to wade/negotiate) shares stroke elements with 商, but 渉売 is not a real Japanese word and does not mean trade or business.

explanation: 商売 means 'trade / business / commerce'; 商 means 'merchant/trade' and 売 means 'to sell', together correctly expressing commercial activity.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 記念 is read きねん (not きにゅう) and means 'commemoration / memory', not 'filling in a form'.
Visual confusion: 寄 is visually similar to 記 due to shared components, but 寄入 is not a real Japanese word and does not mean 'entry on a form'.
Visual confusion: 紀 (chronicle/era) looks similar to 記 (to record) sharing the 己 component, but 紀入 is not a real word and does not mean 'filling in a form'.

explanation: 記入 means 'entry / filling in (a form)'; 記 means 'to record/write down' and 入 means 'to enter/put in', together correctly expressing the act of writing information into a document.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 精工 is read せいこう and refers to precision craftsmanship (as in a brand name context), but 巧 (skillful/elaborate) is the correct character for the meaning 'exquisite in make.'
Homophone trap: 成功 is also read せいこう but means 'success,' not 'elaborate/exquisite.'
Near-homophone trap: 精考 (せいこう) means 'careful thinking/deliberation,' not 'elaborate craftsmanship.'

explanation: 精巧 (せいこう) means 'elaborate and finely crafted'; 精 (refined/detailed) and 巧 (skillful) together correctly describe the intricate mechanical doll in the sentence.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 徹 looks similar to 哲 but means 'penetrate/thorough,' making 徹学 a non-standard word unrelated to philosophy.
Visual confusion: 額 is visually similar to 学 in some fonts/handwriting but means 'forehead/amount,' so 哲額 is not a real word.
Near-homophone trap: 転学 is read てんがく (not てつがく) and means 'transferring schools,' not 'philosophy.'

explanation: 哲学 (てつがく) means 'philosophy'; 哲 (wisdom/sage) combined with 学 (study/learning) correctly names the academic discipline the speaker is studying at university.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 困 is visually similar to 混 but means 'troubled/困る,' so 困乱 is not the standard word for 'chaos.'
Near-homophone trap: 混雑 is read こんざつ (not こんらん) and means 'congestion/crowding,' not 'chaos/confusion.'
Homophone trap: 婚 is read こん (as in marriage) and replaces 混, creating a nonsense compound; 婚乱 is not a real word.

explanation: 混乱 (こんらん) means 'chaos or confusion'; 混 (mixed up) and 乱 (disorder) together correctly describe the traffic disruption caused by construction in the sentence.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 意志 is also read いし but means 'willpower/determination' (inner mental strength), whereas 意思 refers to one's intention or wish as communicated to others.
Homophone trap: 医師 is also read いし but means 'medical doctor,' not 'intention or purpose.'
Near-homophone trap: 意地 is read いじ (not いし) and means 'stubbornness/disposition,' not 'intention.'

explanation: 意思 (いし) means 'intention or purpose as expressed to others'; 意 (mind/thought) and 思 (think/feel) together correctly convey the idea of someone's stated wish that should be respected.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 盤 is visually similar to 板 but refers to a tray or disc (as in 碁盤), not a flat board used in a classroom.
Near-homophone trap: 墨 is read ぼく/すみ (not こく) and means 'ink/sumi,' making 墨板 a non-standard term for a blackboard.
Visual confusion: 版 looks similar to 板 but means 'print/edition' (as in 出版), so 黒版 does not refer to a classroom blackboard.

explanation: 黒板 (こくばん) means 'blackboard'; 黒 (black) and 板 (flat board/plank) together correctly name the surface the teacher writes math problems on.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 起業 is also read きぎょう but means 'starting a business/entrepreneurship,' not an established company or industry.
Homophone trap: 機業 is also read きぎょう and refers specifically to the textile/weaving industry, not companies or businesses in general.
Near-homophone trap: 企画 is read きかく (not きぎょう) and means 'planning/project,' not 'company or enterprise.'

explanation: 企業 (きぎょう) means 'a company or business enterprise'; 企 (plan/undertake) and 業 (industry/work) together correctly refer to the large corporation where the speaker's father works.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 感傷 is read かんしょう (not かんじょう) and means 'sentimentality,' which is related but a different and narrower word.
Homophone trap: 勘定 is also read かんじょう but means 'bill/account/calculation,' not 'emotion or feeling.'
Visual confusion: 状 is visually similar to 情 but means 'letter/condition/state,' so 感状 means 'letter of commendation,' not 'emotion.'

explanation: 感情 (かんじょう) means 'emotions or feelings'; 感 (feeling/sense) and 情 (emotion/sentiment) together correctly describe the inner emotional state that is difficult to understand.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 問屋 is read とんや/といや (not とい alone) and means 'wholesale dealer,' not 'a question.'
Homophone trap: 樋 is also read とい and means 'gutter/drainpipe,' a completely different word from 'question.'
Near-homophone trap: 疑い is read うたがい (not とい) and means 'suspicion/doubt,' not 'a question or query.'

explanation: 問い (とい) means 'a question or query'; 問 (ask/question) is the correct kanji that captures the act of the teacher posing a difficult question to the students.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 離す is also read はなす but means 'to separate/detach (two things apart),' not 'to set free/release.'
Homophone trap: 話す is also read はなす but means 'to speak/talk,' not 'to set free.'
Near-homophone trap: 倣す is read ならす, not はなす, and means 'to imitate/model after.'

explanation: 放す means 'to release or set free,' and 放 (release/set loose) correctly captures the action of letting a bird fly free into the sky.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 内用 is read ないよう but specifically means 'for internal/personal use,' not 'contents or subject matter.'
Near-homophone trap: 内様 is read ないよう (or うちさま) and refers to 'one's wife (honorific),' not 'contents.'
Near-homophone trap: 内納 is read ないのう and means 'paying in private/secretly,' not 'contents'; the reading differs by one mora.

explanation: 内容 means 'contents or subject matter,' and the kanji 内 (inside) + 容 (hold/contain) together correctly express what a book holds within it.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 級 looks similar to 吸 (both share the 及 component) but means 'grade/rank,' making 呼級 a nonsensical combination.
Near-homophone trap: 古休 would be read こきゅう but 古 means 'old' and 休 means 'rest,' making this a meaningless pairing unrelated to breathing.
Near-homophone trap: 鼓 is read こ and means 'drum,' so 鼓吸 is not a real word and does not mean respiration.

explanation: 呼吸 means 'breathing/respiration,' with 呼 (exhale/call) and 吸 (inhale/absorb) together capturing the full cycle of breath.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 重用 is also read じゅうよう but means 'to make heavy use of (a person),' not 'important/essential.'
Near-homophone trap: 充用 is read じゅうよう and means 'appropriation/application of funds,' a different word from 'important.'
Visual confusion: 容 looks similar to 要 but means 'form/appearance,' so 重容 is not a real Japanese word meaning 'important.'

explanation: 重要 means 'important or essential,' with 重 (heavy/weighty) and 要 (necessary) together conveying that something carries great significance.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 前 is read ぜん (or まえ) and means 'before/front,' not 'a meal tray or small dining table.'
Homophone trap: 善 is also read ぜん and means 'goodness/virtue,' not 'a meal tray.'
Homophone trap: 禅 is also read ぜん and means 'Zen Buddhism,' not 'a meal tray or table setting.'

explanation: 膳 means 'a (small) dining tray or table setting for a meal,' and the kanji 膳 specifically refers to the traditional Japanese meal tray used to serve food.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 不死 is also read ふし and means 'immortality,' not 'a musical tune or melody.'
Homophone trap: 父子 is also read ふし and means 'father and child,' not 'a tune or musical phrase.'
Near-homophone trap: 符詞 is read ふじ (not ふし) and refers to 'a coded word/phrase,' differing in both reading and meaning.

explanation: 節 means 'a tune, melody phrase, or musical tone,' and in this context it refers to a beloved musical motif or song phrase that has been popular for a long time.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 人世 is also read じんせい and means 'the human world/society,' a subtly different concept from one's individual lifetime journey.
Homophone trap: 人性 is also read じんせい and means 'human nature,' not 'one's life from birth to death.'
Homophone trap: 人声 is also read じんせい (or ひとごえ) and means 'a human voice,' not 'human life.'

explanation: 人生 means 'one's (human) life from birth to death,' and the kanji 人 (person) + 生 (life) together express the full journey of a person's existence.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 肆 is an archaic/formal kanji also read し (not よん) meaning 'four' in formal counting or 'unrestrained,' and is not used in modern everyday contexts like team size.
Near-homophone trap: 世 is read よ or せい (not よん) and means 'world/generation,' making it wrong in both reading and meaning.
Visual confusion: 囲 looks similar to 四 (both are square-shaped kanji with enclosed structure) but means 'to surround/encircle,' not 'four.'

explanation: 四 means 'four' and is the standard kanji used for the number 4 in Japanese, correctly indicating that the team is composed of four people.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 色 is also read しょく (in compounds) but means 'color,' not 'employment.'
Homophone trap: 食 is also read しょく but means 'food/eating,' not 'employment.'
Near-homophone trap: 植 is read しょく in compounds but means 'planting/vegetation,' not 'employment.'

explanation: 職 means 'employment' or 'post/position,' and 職を得た means 'obtained employment'; 職 is the only kanji that carries the meaning of an occupational post.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 寂 looks somewhat similar to 込 but means 'loneliness/desolation,' not 'crowding.'
Near-homophone trap: 人好み is read ひとごのみ and means 'people's taste/preference,' not 'a crowd of people.'
Visual confusion: 混 (to mix/mingle) looks similar to 込 and is sometimes confused with it, but 人混み, while occasionally seen informally, the standard orthography for this N3 target word is 人込み with 込.

explanation: 人込み means 'a crowd of people' and uses 込 (to be crowded/packed in) to express the sense of people being packed together.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 銘じる is also read めいじる and means 'to engrave/bear in mind,' not 'to order or command.'
Visual confusion: 盟 looks similar to 命 but means 'alliance/oath,' not 'command or order.'
Near-homophone trap: 鳴 is read めい/なる and means 'to ring/cry out,' not 'to order or appoint.'

explanation: 命じる means 'to order, command, or appoint,' and 命 (life/decree) is the correct kanji conveying the sense of issuing an authoritative instruction.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 夜 is read よる/よ (not の) and means 'night,' not 'field.'
Near-homophone trap: 農 is read のう (not の) and means 'agriculture,' not 'field.'
Visual confusion: 埜 is an archaic/variant character for 野 and looks similar, but it is not the standard modern kanji used for 'field.'

explanation: 野 means 'field' or 'open plain,' and is the standard kanji for this reading (の) in the context of a natural open landscape that one walks through.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 考 looks similar to 幸 but means 'to think/consider,' making 不考 a non-standard word unrelated to misfortune.
Homophone trap: 不孝 is also read ふこう and means 'lack of filial piety,' not 'misfortune or unhappiness.'
Near-homophone trap: 負荷 is read ふか (not ふこう) and means 'load/burden,' not 'misfortune.'

explanation: 不幸 means 'unhappiness, misfortune, or sorrow,' and 幸 (happiness/fortune) combined with the negative prefix 不 correctly expresses the concept of misfortune.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 失亡 is read しつぼう but is not a standard word; 亡 means 'death/loss' and does not collocate with 失 to mean 'disappointment.'
Visual confusion: 忘 looks similar to 望 but means 'to forget,' so 失忘 does not express disappointment.
Visual confusion: 室 looks similar to 失 but means 'room/chamber,' making 室望 a non-word unrelated to disappointment.

explanation: 失望 means 'disappointment or despair,' combining 失 (to lose) and 望 (hope/desire) to express the loss of hope.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 早る is not a standard verb in this form; 早 means 'early/fast' and does not capture the meaning of being fashionable or popular.
Near-homophone trap: 映る is read うつる (not はやる) and means 'to be reflected/projected,' not 'to be popular.'
Visual confusion: 栄 looks similar to 流 in brushstroke structure but means 'to flourish/prosper,' and 栄る is not the standard form for 'to be fashionable.'

explanation: 流行る means 'to be popular or come into fashion,' using 流行 (trend/fashion) as its kanji base to express widespread popularity among people.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap — 故人 (こじん) is a real word meaning 'the deceased / a late person,' so it sounds identical but has a completely different meaning.
Near-homophone trap — 古人 (こじん) is a real word meaning 'the ancients / people of old,' sharing the same reading but referring to people from the past, not a living individual.
Visual similarity — 固 closely resembles 個 in overall structure and both share the same reading こ, making this a plausible but incorrect kanji substitution.

explanation: 個人 means 'individual' or 'private person,' and 個 specifically conveys the sense of a single, separate unit of a person, making it the correct character to express personal or individual identity.

distractors.whyWrong: Visual confusion: 図 looks similar to 都 but means 'diagram/plan,' so 図市 is not a word meaning 'city.'
Visual confusion: 使 looks similar to 市 but means 'to use/envoy,' so 都使 does not mean 'city.'
Near-homophone trap: 年市 is read としいち and refers to a 'year-end market,' not 'city' as a general urban concept.

explanation: 都市 means 'city' or 'urban area,' combining 都 (metropolis/capital) and 市 (city/market) to refer to an urban settlement.

distractors.text: 一体で売られています
distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 体 is read たい and 一体 means 'one body/in the first place,' not 'a pair or set.'

distractors.text: 一退で売られています
distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 退 is read たい and 一退 is not a standard word meaning 'pair'; 退 means 'retreat/withdraw.'

distractors.text: 一貸で売られています
distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 貸 is read かし/たい and 一貸 is not a real word; 貸 means 'to lend,' not 'pair or set.'

explanation: 対 means 'pair, couple, or set,' and 一対 specifically means 'one pair/set,' making 対 the correct kanji to express two matching items sold together.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 比 is also read ひ but means 'compare/ratio', not 'criticize/comment on'.
Near-homophone trap: 批平 could suggest へいひょう or びへい and does not mean criticism or review.
Visual confusion: 票 (ballot/vote) looks similar to 評 but means ballot, not commentary.

explanation: 批評 means 'criticism or review'; 批 (to criticize) + 評 (to comment/assess) correctly captures the idea of evaluating something such as a film.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 書く is also read かく but means 'to write', not 'to draw or depict'.
Homophone trap: 欠く is also read かく but means 'to lack/chip', not 'to draw'.
Homophone trap: 掻く is also read かく but means 'to scratch', not 'to depict'.

explanation: 描く means 'to draw or depict'; 描 specifically carries the meaning of portraying or illustrating something visually or in writing, making it the correct kanji in a sketching context.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 固む would suggest かたむ/かたまる (to harden/solidify), not 'to surround'.
Near-homophone trap: 回 is read かい/まわ and relates to 'going around', not encircling/surrounding as a verb.
Visual confusion: 困 looks similar to 囲 due to the outer enclosure radical, but 困 means 'to be troubled/困る', not 'to surround'.

explanation: 囲む means 'to surround or encircle'; the kanji 囲 depicts something enclosed within a boundary, perfectly matching the image of a park surrounded by a tall wall.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 感 is also read かん but means 'feeling/emotion' as a standalone or suffix, while 観 specifically means 'view/outlook/perspective'.
Homophone trap: 管 is also read かん but means 'pipe/tube/control', not 'view or perspective'.
Visual confusion: 勧 looks similar to 観 due to the shared 又 component on the right, but 勧 means 'to recommend/encourage', not 'view/outlook'.

explanation: ～観 means 'view or outlook' (as in 価値観, one's sense of values); 観 carries the meaning of a perspective or way of seeing, which is the correct character for this suffix.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 堅い is read かたい (not かしこい) and means 'hard/firm/rigid', not 'wise or clever'.
Near-homophone trap: 利い would be read きい (from 利く, to be effective), not かしこい, and does not mean 'wise'.
Visual confusion: 堂 looks similar to 賢 in its lower structure, but 堂 means 'hall/temple' and 堂い is not a correct adjective for 'wise'.

explanation: 賢い means 'wise, clever, or smart'; 賢 is the specific kanji for intellectual sharpness or good judgment, making it the correct choice for describing someone who solves difficult problems easily.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 待つ is read まつ (to wait), not みちる, and does not mean 'to be full or to mature'.
Near-homophone trap: 道 is read みち as a noun meaning 'road/path' but 道る is not the correct verb for 'to be full'; it is a different word.
Visual confusion: 溝 (みぞ, gutter/groove) shares a similar water radical and stroke structure with 満 but means 'ditch or groove', not 'to be full'.

explanation: 満ちる means 'to be full or to mature'; 満 carries the sense of being filled to capacity or reaching fullness, correctly describing the arrival of a season's abundance.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 惑 is read わく in compounds (e.g., 迷惑) but as a standalone noun it means 'confusion/bewilderment', not 'frame or border'.
Homophone trap: 沸く is also read わく but is a verb meaning 'to boil/to well up', not a noun meaning 'frame'.
Visual confusion: 柱 (はしら, pillar/column) shares the same 木 radical as 枠 and both relate to structural elements, but 柱 means 'pillar', not 'frame'.

explanation: 枠 means 'frame or border' (e.g., a slide frame in a presentation); the kanji 枠 combines 木 (wood) with an enclosure, evoking a physical frame or boundary.

distractors.whyWrong: Homophone trap: 前 is also read ぜん in compounds (e.g., 前回) but as a standalone means 'before/front', not 'all/whole/entire'.
Homophone trap: 善 is also read ぜん but means 'good/virtue' (as in 善悪), not 'all or whole'.
Homophone trap: 然 is also read ぜん in some contexts (e.g., 天然) but means 'nature/so/thus', not 'all or entire'.

explanation: 全 means 'all, whole, or entire'; it is the correct kanji when expressing totality as in 全ての人 (all people), carrying the specific meaning of completeness or entirety.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 提感 would suggest ていかん, a different reading and meaning (sense of resignation), not 'proposal or suggestion'.
Near-homophone trap: 低案 could be read ていあん but 低 means 'low/inferior', making 低案 mean a 'low-grade plan' rather than the neutral word for 'proposal'.
Visual confusion: 堤 (embankment/levee) looks similar to 提 due to sharing the 是 component, but 堤 means 'embankment' and 堤案 is not the correct word for 'proposal'.

explanation: 提案 means 'proposal or suggestion'; 提 (to present/put forward) + 案 (plan/idea) together precisely express the act of putting a suggestion forward to a group, making this the correct kanji compound.

distractors.whyWrong: Near-homophone trap: 最初 is read さいしょ (not さいしゅう) and means 'first/beginning,' the opposite of 'last.'
